rule_seed: total_daily_vitamin_d_mcg > 100 or total_daily_vitamin_d_iu > 4000 in adults; also flag high serum 25(OH)D if provided
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL 100 mcg/day (4,000 IU); toxicity usually through supplements; serum 25(OH)D >150 ng/mL is a toxicity marker
safety_signal: hypercalcemia; hypercalciuria; kidney stones; renal failure/arrhythmia in severe toxicity
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin D health professional fact sheet
notes: Useful first rule because threshold and outcomes are explicit. Separate supplement dose from therapeutic prescription.

rule_seed: total_calcium_mg > adult UL or supplemental_calcium_mg >= 1000 with stone history/high vitamin D/renal risk
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL generally 2,000-2,500 mg/day by age; WHI signal used for kidney stone concern with 1,000 mg/day supplemental calcium
safety_signal: hypercalcemia; hypercalciuria; nephrolithiasis; constipation; absorption interaction
source_basis: NIH ODS Calcium health professional fact sheet
notes: Model total calcium and supplemental calcium separately; rule should combine dose with kidney stone history and vitamin D co-use.

rule_seed: vitamin_b6_mg > 12 as EU caution; vitamin_b6_mg >= 100 as US adult UL exceedance
threshold_or_trigger: US FNB adult UL 100 mg/day; EFSA 2023 adult UL 12 mg/day based on peripheral neuropathy
safety_signal: peripheral neuropathy; sensory neuropathy; paresthesia; neurotoxicity
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin B6 health professional fact sheet; EFSA B6 UL scientific opinion
notes: Use dual-threshold logic: low caution at EFSA threshold, stronger alert at US UL or long duration.

rule_seed: B-complex product contains B6 above caution threshold or multiple B products with cumulative B6 dose unknown
threshold_or_trigger: Risk depends on pyridoxine equivalent dose and duration; product labels may hide cumulative B6 from multiple products
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin B6 health professional fact sheet
notes: Prompt user to enter per-product B6 amount; do not infer safety from product category alone.

rule_seed: supplemental_magnesium_mg > 350 or magnesium laxative/antacid co-use; renal impairment escalates severity
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL for magnesium from supplements/medications is 350 mg/day; food magnesium is not counted toward this UL
safety_signal: diarrhea; nausea; abdominal cramping; irregular heartbeat/cardiac arrest at extreme intake
source_basis: NIH ODS Magnesium consumer and health professional fact sheets
notes: Rule must use supplemental elemental magnesium, not compound weight; renal impairment should upgrade alert.

rule_seed: total_iron_mg >= 45 unless clearly therapeutic under clinician direction; hemochromatosis or child exposure escalates
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL 45 mg/day; high-dose iron can cause GI adverse effects and reduce zinc absorption
safety_signal: constipation; nausea; abdominal pain; vomiting; diarrhea; zinc absorption reduction
source_basis: NIH ODS Iron consumer and health professional fact sheets
notes: Separate OTC self-use from prescribed iron-deficiency treatment; flag levothyroxine spacing in interaction rules.

rule_seed: total_zinc_mg > 40 or chronic high-dose zinc product/denture adhesive exposure
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL 40 mg/day; excess zinc can cause GI symptoms and copper deficiency
safety_signal: nausea; vomiting; gastric distress; copper deficiency; reduced immune markers
source_basis: NIH ODS Zinc health professional fact sheet
notes: Add chronic-use duration field because copper deficiency is duration-dependent.

rule_seed: preformed_vitamin_a_mcg_rae > 3000 in adults; pregnancy/liver disease/alcohol use escalates
threshold_or_trigger: Adult UL for preformed vitamin A is 3,000 mcg RAE/day; toxicity differs from provitamin A carotenoids
safety_signal: liver toxicity; teratogenic risk; dry skin; bone-related concern
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin A health professional fact sheet
notes: Track preformed retinol separately from beta-carotene/provitamin A; important for multivitamin/cod liver oil examples.

rule_seed: vitamin_e_mg >= 1000 or high-dose vitamin E plus anticoagulant/antiplatelet/low vitamin K
threshold_or_trigger: Adult supplement UL 1,000 mg/day; high doses may increase bleeding risk, especially with anticoagulants/antiplatelets
safety_signal: bleeding; hemorrhagic stroke concern; anticoagulant interaction
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin E health professional fact sheet
notes: Rule should check warfarin/antiplatelet co-use even below UL if dose is high.

rule_seed: warfarin_or_vka_use and sudden vitamin_k_intake_change from supplement
threshold_or_trigger: No established UL signal for typical vitamin K, but clinically important interaction with warfarin-like anticoagulants
safety_signal: reduced or unstable anticoagulant effect; INR instability
source_basis: NIH ODS Vitamin K health professional fact sheet
notes: This belongs in interaction rules, not dose-toxicity rules. Stable intake matters more than absolute high intake.
